spans.text: me fair , and nymphs and swains besought me with a pray’r ; Where e’er I came the maiden ceas’d her grief , And doubting lovers felt a sweet relief
ev’ry month danc’d fairly off his legs
he — dear madam — sweet and fair — Where all the lilies and the roses are , My heart , dear madam — O , could I but tell
rich as Cresus , and perhaps as rotten
with the help of Trim , To knock up Common Sense , both life and limb
’tis clever , But we may write , and write whole years together
the fame they give to him
